message.content:
Read and summarize the contents of these config files for a quality-of-life review. Be thorough and capture all settings, bindings, and notable configurations:

1. ~/.config/hypr/hyprland.conf
2. ~/.config/hypr/bindings.conf
3. ~/.config/hypr/looknfeel.conf
4. ~/.config/hypr/input.conf
5. ~/.config/hypr/monitors.conf
6. ~/.config/hypr/autostart.conf
7. ~/.config/hypr/hypridle.conf
8. ~/.config/hypr/hyprlock.conf
9. ~/.config/alacritty/alacritty.toml
10. ~/.config/waybar/config.jsonc
11. ~/.config/waybar/style.css
12. ~/.config/starship.toml (if exists)

List all file contents in full so I can review them.

content.text: I'll read all these configuration files for you. Let me start by reading them in parallel.

content.content:
1→# Learn how to configure Hyprland: https://wiki.hyprland.org/Configuring/
     2→
     3→# Use defaults Omarchy defaults (but don't edit these directly!)
     4→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/autostart.conf
     5→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/bindings/media.conf
     6→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/bindings/clipboard.conf
     7→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/bindings/tiling-v2.conf
     8→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/bindings/utilities.conf
     9→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/envs.conf
    10→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/looknfeel.conf
    11→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/input.conf
    12→source = ~/.local/share/omarchy/default/hypr/windows.conf
    13→source = ~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/hyprland.conf
    14→
    15→# Change your own setup in these files (and overwrite any settings from defaults!)
    16→source = ~/.config/hypr/monitors.conf
    17→source = ~/.config/hypr/input.conf
    18→source = ~/.config/hypr/bindings.conf
    19→source = ~/.config/hypr/looknfeel.conf
    20→source = ~/.config/hypr/autostart.conf
    21→
    22→# Add any other personal Hyprland configuration below
    23→# windowrule = workspace 5, match:class qemu
    24→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→# Application bindings
     2→bindd = SUPER ALT, RETURN, Tmux, exec, uwsm-app -- xdg-terminal-exec --dir="$(omarchy-cmd-terminal-cwd)" tmux new
     3→$terminal = uwsm-app -- xdg-terminal-exec
     4→$browser = omarchy-launch-browser
     5→
     6→bindd = SUPER, RETURN, Terminal, exec, $terminal --working-directory="$(omarchy-cmd-terminal-cwd)"
     7→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, RETURN, Browser, exec, omarchy-launch-browser
     8→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, F, File manager, exec, uwsm-app -- nautilus --new-window
     9→bindd = SUPER ALT SHIFT, F, File manager (cwd), exec, uwsm-app -- nautilus --new-window "$(omarchy-cmd-terminal-cwd)"
    10→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, B, Browser, exec, $browser
    11→bindd = SUPER SHIFT ALT, B, Browser (private), exec, $browser --private
    12→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, M, Music, exec, omarchy-launch-or-focus spotify
    13→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, N, Editor, exec, omarchy-launch-editor
    14→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, T, Activity, exec, omarchy-launch-tui btop
    15→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, D, Docker, exec, omarchy-launch-tui lazydocker
    16→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, G, Signal, exec, omarchy-launch-or-focus signal "uwsm-app -- signal-desktop"
    17→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, Q, Obsidian, exec, omarchy-launch-or-focus "^obsidian$" "uwsm-app -- obsidian -disable-gpu --enable-wayland-ime"
    18→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, W, Typora, exec, uwsm-app -- typora --enable-wayland-ime
    19→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, SLASH, Passwords, exec, uwsm-app -- 1password
    20→
    21→# If your web app url contains #, type it as ## to prevent hyprland treating it as a comment
    22→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, A, ChatGPT, exec, omarchy-launch-webapp "https://chatgpt.com"
    23→bindd = SUPER SHIFT ALT, A, Grok, exec, omarchy-launch-webapp "https://grok.com"
    24→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, C, Calendar, exec, omarchy-launch-webapp "https://app.hey.com/calendar/weeks/"
    25→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, E, Email, exec, omarchy-launch-webapp "https://app.hey.com"
    26→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, Y, YouTube, exec, omarchy-launch-webapp "https://youtube.com/"
    27→bindd = SUPER SHIFT ALT, G, WhatsApp, exec, omarchy-launch-or-focus-webapp WhatsApp "https://web.whatsapp.com/"
    28→bindd = SUPER SHIFT CTRL, G, Google Messages, exec, omarchy-launch-or-focus-webapp "Google Messages" "https://messages.google.com/web/conversations"
    29→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, P, Google Photos, exec, omarchy-launch-or-focus-webapp "Google Photos" "https://photos.google.com/"
    30→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, X, X, exec, omarchy-launch-webapp "https://x.com/"
    31→bindd = SUPER SHIFT ALT, X, X Post, exec, omarchy-launch-webapp "https://x.com/compose/post"
    32→
    33→# Overwrite existing bindings, like putting Omarchy Menu on Super + Space
    34→# unbind = SUPER, SPACE
    35→# bindd = SUPER, SPACE, Omarchy menu, exec, omarchy-menu
    36→#
    37→bindd = SUPER, C, Class Peek, exec, omarchy-launch-tui bash -c "hypr-classpeek; read -p 'Press Enter to close…'"
    38→bindd = SUPER ALT, C, Class Peek (active), exec, omarchy-launch-tui hypr-classpeek --active
    39→
    40→
    41→# Office layout recovery
    42→bind = SUPER SHIFT, O, exec, ~/.config/hypr/scripts/office-layout.sh
    43→
    44→# Home layout recovery
    45→bind = SUPER SHIFT, H, exec, ~/.config/hypr/scripts/home-layout.sh
    46→
    47→
    48→bindd = SUPER, F1, Cheat Sheet, exec, omarchy-launch-tui bash -c "less -R ~/.cheatsheet"
    49→bindd = SUPER SHIFT, F1, Cheat Sheet (edit), exec, omarchy-launch-tui nvim ~/.cheatsheet
    50→
    51→
    52→
    53→

</system-reminder>

1→# Change the default Omarchy look'n'feel
     2→
     3→# https://wiki.hyprland.org/Configuring/Variables/#general
     4→general {
     5→    # No gaps between windows or borders
     6→    # gaps_in = 0
     7→    # gaps_out = 0
     8→    # border_size = 0
     9→
    10→    # Use master layout instead of dwindle
    11→    # layout = master
    12→}
    13→
    14→# https://wiki.hyprland.org/Configuring/Variables/#decoration
    15→decoration {
    16→    # Use round window corners
    17→    # rounding = 8
    18→}
    19→
    20→# https://wiki.hypr.land/Configuring/Variables/#layout
    21→layout {
    22→    # Avoid overly wide single-window layouts on wide screens
    23→    # single_window_aspect_ratio = 1 1
    24→}
    25→
    26→cursor {
    27→    no_hardware_cursors = true
    28→  }
    29→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→# Control your input devices
     2→# See https://wiki.hypr.land/Configuring/Variables/#input
     3→input {
     4→  # Use multiple keyboard layouts and switch between them with Left Alt + Right Alt
     5→  # kb_layout = us,dk,eu
     6→  kb_options = compose:caps # ,grp:alts_toggle
     7→
     8→  # Change speed of keyboard repeat
     9→  repeat_rate = 40
    10→  repeat_delay = 600
    11→
    12→  # Start with numlock on by default
    13→  numlock_by_default = true
    14→
    15→  # Increase sensitivity for mouse/trackpad (default: 0)
    16→  # sensitivity = 0.35
    17→
    18→  touchpad {
    19→    # Use natural (inverse) scrolling
    20→    # natural_scroll = true
    21→
    22→    # Use two-finger clicks for right-click instead of lower-right corner
    23→    # clickfinger_behavior = true
    24→
    25→    # Control the speed of your scrolling
    26→    scroll_factor = 0.4
    27→
    28→    # Enable the touchpad while typing
    29→    # disable_while_typing = false
    30→
    31→    # Left-click-and-drag with three fingers
    32→    # drag_3fg = 1
    33→  }
    34→}
    35→
    36→# Scroll nicely in the terminal
    37→windowrule = match:class (Alacritty|kitty), scroll_touchpad 1.5
    38→windowrule = match:class com.mitchellh.ghostty, scroll_touchpad 0.2
    39→
    40→# Enable touchpad gestures for changing workspaces
    41→# See https://wiki.hyprland.org/Configuring/Gestures/
    42→# gesture = 3, horizontal, workspace
    43→

<system-reminder>

</system-reminder>

1→
     2→
     3→###############################################################################
     4→# Hyprland Monitor Configuration — LAST KNOWN WORKING STATE
     5→###############################################################################
     6→
     7→# IMPORTANT:
     8→# Do NOT enable auto layout fallback
     9→# monitor=,preferred,auto,auto
    10→
    11→
    12→###############################################################################

    14→###############################################################################
    15→
    16→# Home 4K ViewSonic
    17→ monitor=desc:VX3211-4K,3840x2160@60,0x0,1.5,transform,0

    20→ monitor=eDP-1,2560x1600@165,2560x440,1.6,transform,0

    26→

    32→# Bottom office monitor (DP-9)
    33→monitor=desc:VC42235Q2258,1920x1080@60,0x0,1,transform,0
    34→
    35→# Top office monitor (DP-10), flipped 180°, stacked ABOVE DP-9
    36→monitor=DP-10,1920x1080@60,0x-1080,1,transform,2
    37→

    39→monitor=eDP-1,2560x1600@165,1920x-580,1.6,transform,0
    40→
    41→
    42→###############################################################################
    43→# END OF FILE
    44→###############################################################################

1→general.import = [ "~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/alacritty.toml" ]
     2→
     3→[env]
     4→TERM = "xterm-256color"
     5→
     6→
     7→[font]
     8→size = 11.0
     9→
    10→[font.normal]
    11→family = "JetBrainsMono Nerd Font"
    12→style = "Regular"
    13→
    14→[font.bold]
    15→family = "JetBrainsMono Nerd Font"
    16→style = "Bold"
    17→

    19→family = "JetBrainsMono Nerd Font"
    20→style = "Italic"
    21→

    23→family = "JetBrainsMono Nerd Font"
    24→style = "Bold Italic"
    25→
    26→

    30→{ key = "Insert", mods = "Control", action = "Copy" },
    31→{ key = "Return", mods = "Shift", chars = "\u001B\r" }

1→# --------------------------------------------------
     2→# Starship Prompt — Clean Admin / Dev Configuration
     3→# --------------------------------------------------
     4→
     5→add_newline = true
     6→command_timeout = 200
     7→
     8→# Prompt layout (single line)
     9→format = "$directory$git_branch$git_status$python$character"
    10→
    11→# --------------------------------------------------
    12→# Prompt character
    13→# --------------------------------------------------
    14→[character]
    15→success_symbol = "[❯](bold cyan)"
    16→error_symbol   = "[✗](bold red)"
    17→vimcmd_symbol  = "[❮](bold yellow)"
    18→
    19→# --------------------------------------------------
    20→# Directory
    21→# --------------------------------------------------
    22→[directory]
    23→style = "bold blue"
    24→truncation_length = 3
    25→truncate_to_repo = true
    26→truncation_symbol = "…/"
    27→read_only = " "
    28→read_only_style = "bold red"
    29→
    30→# --------------------------------------------------
    31→# Git
    32→# --------------------------------------------------
    33→[git_branch]
    34→symbol = " "
    35→style = "bold cyan"
    36→format = "[$symbol$branch]($style) "
    37→
    38→[git_status]
    39→style = "yellow"
    40→format = "[$all_status]($style)"
    41→
    42→ahead      = "⇡${count} "
    43→behind     = "⇣${count} "
    44→diverged   = "⇕⇡${ahead_count}⇣${behind_count} "
    45→conflicted = " "
    46→untracked  = "? "
    47→modified   = " "
    48→staged     = "● "
    49→renamed    = "➜ "
    50→deleted    = "✗ "
    51→stashed    = "⚑ "
    52→up_to_date = " "
    53→
    54→# --------------------------------------------------
    55→# Python (critical for your workflow)
    56→# --------------------------------------------------
    57→[python]
    58→symbol = "🐍 "
    59→style = "bold green"
    60→format = "[$symbol$virtualenv]($style) "
    61→
    62→# --------------------------------------------------
    63→# Command duration (only shows when slow)
    64→# --------------------------------------------------
    65→[cmd_duration]
    66→min_time = 2000
    67→style = "dimmed white"
    68→
    69→

content.text:
Perfect! I've read all the configuration files. Here's a comprehensive summary for your quality-of-life review:

---

## HYPRLAND WINDOW MANAGER CONFIGURATION

### 1. **hyprland.conf** - Main Configuration Hub
- Acts as a source aggregator pulling settings from:
  - Default Omarchy settings (autostart, bindings, look & feel, input, windows)
  - Current theme configuration
  - Custom overrides in separate files
- Minimal custom modifications; primarily relying on Omarchy framework defaults

### 2. **bindings.conf** - Extensive Keyboard Shortcuts
**Application Launchers:**
- Super+Return: Terminal (respects working directory)
- Super+Alt+Return: Tmux in new terminal
- Super+Shift+Return: Browser
- Super+Shift+F: File manager (Nautilus)
- Super+Alt+Shift+F: File manager at current working directory

**Quick Access Applications:**
- Super+Shift+B: Browser
- Super+Shift+Alt+B: Private browsing
- Super+Shift+M: Spotify
- Super+Shift+N: Editor
- Super+Shift+T: btop (system activity)
- Super+Shift+D: lazydocker
- Super+Shift+G: Signal
- Super+Shift+Q: Obsidian
- Super+Shift+W: Typora (Markdown editor)
- Super+Shift+Slash: 1Password

**Web App Quick Links:**
- Super+Shift+A: ChatGPT
- Super+Shift+Alt+A: Grok
- Super+Shift+C: Calendar (Hey.com)
- Super+Shift+E: Email (Hey.com)
- Super+Shift+Y: YouTube
- Super+Shift+Alt+G: WhatsApp Web
- Super+Shift+Ctrl+G: Google Messages
- Super+Shift+P: Google Photos
- Super+Shift+X: X (Twitter)
- Super+Shift+Alt+X: X Post composer

**Utilities:**
- Super+C: Class Peek (inspect window class)
- Super+Alt+C: Class Peek (active window only)
- Super+F1: Cheat sheet viewer
- Super+Shift+F1: Cheat sheet editor (Neovim)
- Super+Shift+O: Office layout recovery script
- Super+Shift+H: Home layout recovery script

### 3. **looknfeel.conf** - Visual Customization (Mostly Defaults)
- **General Settings:** Commented out (using defaults - gaps, borders, layout)
- **Decoration:** Round corners disabled (using defaults)
- **Layout:** Single-window aspect ratio constraints disabled
- **Cursor:** Hardware cursors disabled (software cursors only)

### 4. **input.conf** - Input Device Configuration
**Keyboard:**
- Compose key mapped to Caps Lock
- Repeat rate: 40 Hz
- Repeat delay: 600ms
- Numlock on by default

**Touchpad/Mouse:**
- Scroll factor: 0.4 (slower scrolling)
- Natural scrolling disabled
- Two-finger right-click disabled
- Touchpad drag disabled

**Scroll Optimization by Application:**
- Alacritty/Kitty: 1.5x scroll factor
- Ghostty: 0.2x scroll factor

**Note:** Touchpad gestures for workspace navigation commented out

### 5. **monitors.conf** - Multi-Monitor Setup with Two Profiles

**HOME SETUP (HDMI 4K):**
- ViewSonic 4K Monitor: 3840x2160@60Hz at 1.5 scaling, position (0,0)
- Laptop (eDP-1): 2560x1600@165Hz at 1.6 scaling, positioned right at (2560,440)

**OFFICE SETUP (Stable, Working):**
- Bottom Monitor (DP-9): 1920x1080@60Hz, 1x scale at (0,0)
- Top Monitor (DP-10): 1920x1080@60Hz, rotated 180°, positioned above at (0,-1080)
- Laptop: 2560x1600@165Hz at 1.6 scale, positioned right at (1920,-580)

**Note:** Auto layout fallback explicitly disabled

### 6. **autostart.conf** - Startup Processes
- Currently empty; comment showing format for adding custom services via uwsm-app

### 7. **hypridle.conf** - Idle/Sleep Behavior
**Lock & Sleep Settings:**
- Lock command: omarchy-lock-screen
- Pre-sleep: Lock session via loginctl
- Post-sleep: 1s delay + DPMS on
- Sleep inhibit timeout: 3s (until locked)

**Idle Timeline:**
- **150s (2.5min):** Screensaver starts (if hyprlock not active)
- **151s (2.5min):** Lock screen
- **330s (5.5min):** Keyboard backlight off (and restore on resume)
- **330s (5.5min):** Display off via DPMS (and restore on resume)

---

## TERMINAL & SHELL CONFIGURATION

### 8. **alacritty.toml** - Terminal Emulator Settings
**General:**
- Imports theme from Omarchy
- TERM environment: xterm-256color

**Font:**
- Family: JetBrainsMono Nerd Font (all variants)
- Size: 11.0pt
- Styles: Regular, Bold, Italic, Bold Italic

**Keyboard Bindings:**
- Shift+Insert: Paste
- Ctrl+Insert: Copy
- Shift+Return: Escape sequence (for vim modes)

**Terminal:**
- OSC52 (clipboard via escape sequences): Enabled

---

## WAYBAR CONFIGURATION (Status Bar)

### 9. **config.jsonc** - Waybar Module Configuration
**Structure:**
- Position: Top of screen
- Height: 26px
- Reload on style changes: Enabled

**Module Layout:**
- **Left:** Omarchy menu + Hyprland workspaces (1-10, with custom icons)
- **Center:** Clock, update indicator, Voxtype, screen recording, idle status, notification silencing
- **Right:** Tray expander, Bluetooth, Network, Audio, CPU, Battery

**Key Modules:**
- **Workspaces:** Click to activate; persistent workspaces 1-5
- **Clock:** Format "Day HH:MM" (L%A %H:%M); right-click for timezone selector
- **Network:** WiFi speed indicators with bandwidth display; interval 3s
- **Battery:** Shows capacity%, charging animations, warning at 20%, critical at 10%
- **Audio:** Volume icons, mutable, scroll step 5%
- **CPU:** Interval 5s; click opens btop
- **Bluetooth:** Shows connection count
- **Custom Indicators:** Screen recording, voice-to-text status, idle mode, notification silencing (with click toggles)

**Tray:** Icon size 12px, spacing 17px

### 10. **style.css** - Waybar Styling
**General:**
- Font: CaskaydiaMono Nerd Font, 12px
- Background: Imported from Omarchy theme
- Margins: 8px left/right
- No borders or rounding

**Specific Styling:**
- Workspaces: 6px padding, 1.5px margins, empty workspaces at 50% opacity
- Modules spacing: 7.5-17px margins (tray 16px, Bluetooth 17px, network 13px)
- Indicators: Font size 10px, left margin 5px
- Active indicators (screen recording, idle, notification silencing): Red color (#a55555)
- Voxtype recording state: Red color (#a55555)
- Tooltips: 2px padding

---

## SHELL PROMPT

### 11. **starship.toml** - Prompt Configuration
**General Settings:**
- Add newline: Enabled
- Command timeout: 200ms
- Format: Single-line layout

**Prompt Format:** `$directory$git_branch$git_status$python$character`

**Character Symbols:**
- Success: ❯ (cyan)
- Error: ✗ (red)
- Vim command: ❮ (yellow)

**Directory:**
- Style: Bold blue
- Truncation: Last 3 components, truncate to repo root
- Truncation symbol: …/
- Read-only files: Bold red warning

**Git:**
- Branch symbol: (git)
- Branch style: Bold cyan
- Status symbols:
  - Ahead: ⇡${count}
  - Behind: ⇣${count}
  - Diverged: ⇕⇡${ahead_count}⇣${behind_count}
  - Conflicted: 
  - Untracked: ?
  - Modified: 
  - Staged: ●
  - Renamed: ➜
  - Deleted: ✗
  - Stashed: ⚑
  - Up-to-date: ✔

**Python:**
- Symbol: 🐍
- Style: Bold green
- Shows virtual environment names
- Enabled for critical workflow

**Command Duration:**
- Minimum 2s to display (prevents clutter on fast commands)
- Style: Dimmed white

---

## QUALITY-OF-LIFE OBSERVATIONS

**Strengths:**
1. Well-structured modular configuration using Omarchy framework
2. Extensive application launcher bindings for quick access to tools
3. Thoughtful monitor configuration with separate home/office profiles
4. Idle behavior well-tuned (screensaver → lock → sleep progression)
5. Clean, minimalist visual design
6. Practical keyboard settings (compose key, numlock, repeat rates)
7. Shell prompt focused on relevant info (git, directory, python venv)
8. Waybar efficiently displays essential system info with smart grouping

**Areas to Consider:**
1. Touchpad scroll factor (0.4) is quite slow—verify if comfortable
2. Keyboard repeat delay (600ms) may feel slightly sluggish for rapid typing
3. Display timeout (5.5min) is relatively quick; adjust if disruptive
4. Monitor auto-layout disabled—ensure manual setup is always stable
5. No gesture support enabled—swipe-based workspace navigation unavailable
6. Omarchy dependency means custom tweaks require working around framework
